title: trichophyllum mutatum
side_effects: Trichophyllum mutatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure may lead to skin irritation and inflammation. Improper use of the plant may also disrupt the body's natural hormonal balance, potentially causing anxiety, insomnia, and mood swings. Furthermore, interacting with other medications can increase the risk of adverse reactions, such as increased blood pressure or seizures.
